intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Eremiris biglumis". However, I found information on "Eremiris bigelovii" and "Eremophila bigelovii" (also known as "Bigelow's emu bush"). 

Here is a paragraph for Eremophila bigelovii:

Eremophila bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's emu bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Eremophila bigelovii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Eremophila bigelovii include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically or internally to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
